title: alnus hirsuta var. microphylla
intro: Alnus hirsuta var. microphylla, also known as the dwarf alder, is a rare and small-statured deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the alder family (Betulaceae). Traditionally, Alnus hirsuta var. microphylla has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits associated with this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function. Alnus hirsuta var. microphylla can be prepared in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are made by extracting its active compounds through methods like steam distillation or solvent extraction.
